channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Collaboration critical for insurers, brokers, risk managers to navigate rapidly evolving risks, warns Markel leader
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: India’s GIC Re appoints Hitesh Joshi as chairman, MD
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Zurich Italy hosts first AI hackathon with Microsoft
item.description: Zurich Italy has launched its first AI Hackathon, bringing together around 40 employees from across the business to develop practical artificial intelligence applications in collaboration with Microsoft. The three-day initiative formed part of Zurich’s broader effort to build internal AI capabilities and promote what the insurer described as a culture of “conscious innovation.” Participants from underwriting, distribution, claims, life, and operations worked in cross-functional teams to develop AI agent prototypes addressing real business use cases, with support from Microsoft solution architects. The hackathon followed the establishment of an internal network of AI Ambassadors, representing different business functions, and an 11-hour training program delivered by Microsoft to explore AI opportunities and applications.

item.title: Compartir información y tokenizar datos: las apuestas para blindar los pagos
item.description: Así, el intercambio de información entre las instituciones gana terreno como un mecanismo para identificar patrones maliciosos y prevenir esquemas como las cuentas mula, la falsificación de identidad y el robo de credenciales Accede al análisis que impulsa la toma de decisiones en la banca latinoamericana Este contenido exclusivo está disponible solo para miembros registrados de iupana. Regístrate gratis o inicia sesión para leer el artículo completo y descubrir: Perspectivas estratégicas sobre innovación, regulación y tendencias en banca digital y fintech Entrevistas con líderes que están transformando el sector financiero en la región Reportes y estudios que anticipan los cambios clave del mercado Acceso prioritario a información para tomar decisiones informadas y fortalecer tu liderazgo Únete a la comunidad de ejecutivos que marcan la diferencia en la transformación digital de la banca. Con los ciberdelincuentes buscando cualquier brecha en el ecosistema financiero para ejecutar robos, la industria de servicios bancarios y de pago ha encontrado en la colaboración una vía para combatir el fraude.

item.title: Judge Dismisses Musk’s xAI Trade Secret Lawsuit Against OpenAI
item.description: A federal judge dismissed a lawsuit on Monday by Elon Musk’s artificial intelligence company xAI that accused rival Sam Altman’s OpenAI of stealing trade secrets for chatbots. Monday’s decision is Musk’s second legal loss against OpenAI in four weeks. On May 18, a federal jury ruled against the world’s richest person in his $150 billion lawsuit accusing OpenAI and Altman of “stealing a charity” by betraying the company’s original mission as a nonprofit to enrich themselves.

item.title: SpaceX Acquires AI Coding Tool Cursor For $60B In Year’s Largest Startup M&A Deal
item.description: SpaceX , fresh off its record-breaking IPO, formalized plans to purchase the startup behind the popular AI coding tool Cursor for $60 billion in an all-stock deal, marking one of the largest acquisitions of a venture-backed startup in recent years and the biggest so far in 2026. The acquisition gives SpaceX, which raised $75 billion in its IPO last week , a foothold into the enterprise software development market, where AI-assisted coding has taken off and led large companies to significantly pare back their reliance on human engineers. Cursor said in November last year that it had crossed $1 billion in annualized revenue.

item.title: People Moves: Lockton Appoints Lespérance as CEO of Canadian Business; Aon Taps Marsh’s Desmond as Head of Construction for UK Commercial Risk
item.description: Lockton Appoints Lespérance as CEO of Canadian Business Lockton , the privately held independent insurance brokerage, announced the appointment of industry veteran Stéphane Lespérance as chief executive officer of Lockton’s new Canadian business. The appointment reflects Lockton’s focus on building a Canadian-led organization to support businesses navigating a dynamic operating environment, with rising pressure across risk and people solutions. This edition of International People Moves details appointments at the brokers Lockton and Aon.

item.title: Russian Warship Fires Warning Shots Near British Yacht
item.description: The UK is investigating the firing of warning shots by a Russian warship near a British yacht just outside of its territorial waters. After this, the British-flagged yacht immediately changed course and moved away from the Russian warship,” the Russian Defense Ministry said in the statement on Telegram. The UK received reports from a UK-registered yacht alleging Russia’s Admiral Grigorovich-class frigate fired the shots at a distance of approximately 500 yards (457 meters), near a point 20 nautical miles (37 kilometers) south of the Isle of Wight.

item.title: Inszone acquires 111-year-old Massachusetts agency
item.description: Inszone Insurance announced the acquisition of Sanford, Bruker & Banks, an independent insurance agency founded in 1915. Following the transaction, the agency’s employees will remain in place and continue serving clients under Inszone’s ownership. The deal adds another long-established agency to Inszone’s growing acquisition portfolio, reflecting the continued wave of succession-driven consolidation across the independent agency channel as aging owners seek partners to ensure continuity for employees and customers.

item.title: Suntex Marinas partners with NFP
item.description: Suntex Marinas, a national owner and operator of marina properties, announced a partnership with NFP to provide a more “refined and customer-centric” boat and yacht insurance program for Suntex customers across the United States. Suntex has a network of over 100 marinas nationwide. Powered by a multi-carrier model, the program delivers a single access point to comprehensive insurance solutions for our customers.” – Bert Fowles, Vice President of Brand and Marketing, Suntex Marinas.

item.title: Akur8 Joins Verisk Alliance Network
item.description: (Image source: Akur8 website.) Akur8 (Paris) has joined Verisk ’s (Jersey City, N.J.) Strategic Alliance partner network and is licensed to support customer-imported Verisk ISO Electronic Rating Content for use with Akur8’s Deploy rating engine. Akur8 says joining the Verisk Strategic Alliance network strengthens its ability to support insurers that rely on licensed ISO ERC content in rating workflows. “By joining the Verisk Strategic Alliance partner network, Akur8 strengthens its ability to support insurers that rely on licensed ISO ERC content as part of their broader rating operations.” Akur8 Acquires Slope Software to Expand into Life Insurance

item.title: Danone US Sues Chobani Over High-Protein Yogurt Labeling Claims
item.description: The lawsuit, filed in the Southern District of New York, claims that Chobani is manipulating the serving size listed on its 32-ounce container of yogurt to make it appear to have a comparable amount of protein as Danone’s Oikos Pro. “If the Chobani Product were truthfully named, labeled and marketed, consumers would not see it as a viable alternative to Oikos Pro in the ultra-high-protein yogurt category,” Danone wrote in its complaint. Danone SA’s US subsidiary sued Chobani in US court on Monday, alleging that Chobani is improperly marketing how much protein is in a serving of its yogurt.

item.title: Ship Owner Seeks Dismissal of All Economic Loss Claims From Baltimore Bridge Collapse
item.description: The city of Baltimore and about 11 private entities including dockworkers and small businesses should not be allowed to collect for any economic losses from the collapse of Baltimore’s Key Bridge in 2024, the owner and manager of the Dali cargo ship that caused the collapse told a federal court. Since the economic loss claimants did not have an ownership interest in the Key Bridge, and have failed to identify a recognizable interest in any other property that sustained physical damage as a result of the casualty, their claims “must all be dismissed as a matter of law,” the marine firms told the court in a filing on June 15. Workers’ Families, Other Parties Settle Baltimore Bridge Tragedy Claims However, the public and private claims for economic losses have not been settled.

item.title: Maintenance, Weak Safety Often Cited in Skydiving Plane Crash Probes
item.description: Poor maintenance is often a factor when skydiving planes crash, as one did in Missouri on Sunday, and the National Transportation Safety Board has said in past investigations that weak oversight of the skydiving industry can allow problems to go unnoticed. Aviation safety expert Jeff Guzzetti said that too often, a skydiving crash investigation reveals lax maintenance and a weak safety culture. Just two years ago, another skydiving plane crashed near the same airport, but in that case, everyone aboard was able to parachute to safety beforehand.

item.title: U.S. Senators Cite Reuters Report in Request for Review of Tesla’s ‘Full Self-Driving’ Safety Data
item.description: senators are asking the nation’s traffic safety regulator to examine Tesla’s self-published crash statistics for its “Full Self-Driving” (FSD) driver-assistance system, following a Reuters investigation last month that found the EV maker was exaggerating its safety claims. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) on Monday, citing the Reuters report and saying the analysis underlying Tesla’s FSD safety statistics is “weak and misleading,” creating “an urgent safety problem.” The letter asks NHTSA to write back by July 7 to answer a series of questions, including whether the agency has evaluated Tesla’s FSD safety claims or asked for the underlying crash data used to make the claims. The senators also urge NHTSA to strengthen reporting requirements for companies using self-driving technology or advanced driver-assistance systems like Tesla’s FSD, saying the agency has no way of knowing whether “public safety claims bear any relationship to reality.” Tesla and NHTSA did not respond to requests for comment.

item.title: Complex Cats, Talent Exodus Will Confound Insurance Models This Year: Report
item.description: “2026 is shaping up to be one of the most complex catastrophe seasons that carriers have ever faced, and the response models most carriers have in place aren’t built for this new reality. The report outlines how current disaster models are outdated, given changes in frequency, type, and location of catastrophes occurring in North America today. The 2026 catastrophe season will face a more distributed and harder-to-predict risk landscape than carriers have historically planned for, according to a new report by global claims administrator Sedgwick.

item.title: Liberty Mutual hands Defne Turkes additional global president, MD role
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Silicon Is Back: Playground Global’s Decade-Long Bet On Hardware, Energy And Deep Tech Looks Prescient
item.description: Playground Global was investing elsewhere: in semiconductors, quantum computing, robotics and energy infrastructure. For much of the past decade, Silicon Valley chased software and apps. The firm recently closed a $475 million fund focused on investing in deep-tech startups at seed and Series A.

item.title: Bringing underwriting and capital to one system enables faster, high-conviction decisions: Peter McKelvy, Northern Re
item.description: During a recent interview with Artemis, Peter McKelvy Co-Founder of Northern Re, a collateralized reinsurer that provides investors with access to the returns of its long-tail casualty underwriting business, explains the goal is to let multiple teams look at the exact same data, allowing them to iterate together and reach a single conclusion much faster. With AI moving at a rapid pace, McKelvy stated that Northern Re embraces the technology, but is cautious about blind automation, and noted that within casualty reinsurance, accuracy, and accountability matter too much to remove human judgement from critical decisions. As market volatility continues to drive a rising demand for structured solutions, reinsurers are increasingly forced to handle complex client objectives all at once, and being able to successfully execute these deals requires underwriting, pricing, and capital teams to be well aligned from the very start.

item.title: El Niño drives redistribution of risk across re/insurance and ILS markets: Twelve Securis
item.description: With the development of a strong El Niño expected to be one of the key drivers of global catastrophe risk during the second half of 2026 and into early 2027, specialist catastrophe bond and insurance-linked securities (ILS) manager Twelve Securis believes that it will also drive a redistribution of risk across the re/insurance and ILS markets. However, Twelve Securis emphasises that from an ILS perspective, El Niño represents a redistribution of risk rather than a simple reduction in risk, altering the geographic concentration and timing of potential losses. In its 2026 Atlantic Hurricane Season outlook, Twelve Securis highlights the climate-conditioned risks that El Niño brings, and the implications that this can have on the ILS market.

item.title: Hippo secures $777m total aggregate reinsurance limit at renewal, $513m for first-event
item.description: has completed the placement of its 2026 reinsurance program, effective June 1st, 2026, securing a meaningful $777 million of total aggregate reinsurance limit, and $513 million of first-event coverage limit. We’ve secured meaningful protection, improved our capital efficiency, and brought in new tools like the whole account quota share that give us flexibility as we grow.” As mentioned, Hippo’s catastrophe protection program provides a first-event coverage limit of $513 million and an aggregate reinsurance coverage limit of $777 million. Rick McCathron, President and CEO of Hippo Holdings, commented: “This renewal is another reflection of how we’re building Hippo for the world as it is today — volatile, fast-moving, and unforgiving of companies that aren’t positioned to respond.

item.title: Marsh Nexus to help firms counter rising international employee benefit costs
item.description: Marsh, a risk advisory, insurance broking and reinsurance services firm, has launched a new captive insurance solution, Marsh Nexus Captive Solution (Marsh Nexus), designed to support multinational organisations in managing risks and costs associated with soaring international employee benefit costs. “By reducing the administrative effort and costs associated with utilizing a single parent captive, Marsh Nexus is a cost-efficient vehicle that complements broader benefits strategies and supports multinational organizations in optimizing next-generation employee benefit programs.” Donna Weber, Marsh Captive Solutions’ Global Pooling and Cell Facilities Leader, added: “The use of captives for international employee benefits has more than doubled in the last five years, and more than 140 companies now have in excess of $3 billion in premium. Paul Lewis, Mercer Marsh Benefits’ Multinational Advisory Growth Leader, commented: “With the continued trend of rising global healthcare costs, organizations are focused on how to balance cost management while still providing valued, personalized employee benefit programs across the world.

item.title: Guy Carpenter appoints Connie Tregidga as MD in Global Structured Solutions
item.description: Guy Carpenter, a Marsh business, has announced the appointment of Connie Tregidga as Managing Director within its Global Structured Solutions team. Commenting on the appointment, Guy Carpenter stated: “We’re pleased to announce that Connie Tregidga has joined Guy Carpenter as a Managing Director in our Global Structured Solutions team. Based in London, Connie will lead on legacy deals and sidecar activity, strengthening our capabilities in two areas that continue to grow in strategic importance.” Guy Carpenter added: “With a background that combines legal training and extensive transaction experience, Connie brings additional depth to our structured solutions offering as we support clients through changing market dynamics.”

item.title: Aon’s new AI platform to help insurers rapidly assess exclusions in reinsurance contracts
item.description: Aon, a major global professional services and consulting firm, has launched a new artificial intelligence (AI) platform, Contract AI, designed to enhance contract certainty through the rapid analysis of reinsurance coverage. The tool enables Aon’s reinsurance brokers to evaluate evolving clauses, exclusions and broader market trends to negotiate and optimize cover for insurer clients. and Canada contract database from the past three years in real-time to identify global coverage trends, limitations, and exclusions.

item.title: People Moves: Lawley Expands Team Across Its Footprint
item.description: Lawley , headquartered in Buffalo, New York, named three new team members. Prior to Lawley, Hoctor was an executive sales representative at Paycom, where he worked with organizations across the state to streamline backend processes and deliver measurable return on investment. Prior to Lawley, Schwartz worked closely with clients and patients in physical therapy, massage therapy, medical aesthetics and health insurance.

item.title: Alabama GOP Dismisses Tuberville Residency Challenge Brought by Insurance Agent
item.description: The unanimous decision came after the party’s 21-member steering committee heard a challenge filed by Tuberville’s former primary opponent, Ken McFeeters, a longtime insurance agency owner in north Alabama. The challenge argued that Tuberville did not meet the Alabama Constitution’s seven-year residency requirement. “I’m thankful for the committee holding a hearing today to finally put this ridiculous residency hoax to bed,” Tuberville said.

item.title: ANV Agrees to Buy Open Lending Corp in All-Cash Deal
item.description: ANV said it will acquire all outstanding shares of Open Lending common stock for $3.15 per share through an all-cash tender offer. said it has entered a definitive merger agreement with Open Lending Corporation. The transaction price represents a premium of about 78% to Open Lending’s 90-day volume weighted average price as of June 15.

item.title: ALPS launches ChatGPT app for lawyers professional liability premium estimates
item.description: ALPS launched a ChatGPT app that allows attorneys to estimate professional liability insurance premiums. The app, ALPS Insurance – Calculate lawyers’ premiums, provides a non-binding range of lawyers professional liability insurance premiums based on a handful of inputs, including the attorney’s state, practice area, and attorney count. ALPS said its cyber rating manual had not been updated since 2021 and that its longstanding practice of charging a uniform premium per attorney no longer adequately reflected the higher exposures faced by larger firms.

item.title: Marsh launches Marsh Nexus Captive Solution
item.description: Marsh announced the launch of Marsh Nexus Captive Solution, a captive insurance offering which supports multinational organizations in managing the risks and costs associated with their international employee benefits programs. Marsh Nexus, which operates through Marsh’s Mangrove Protected Cell Company domiciled in Washington, D.C., is a cell captive structure designed for clients with an employee benefits spend outside the US of more than $3 million annually. By reducing the administrative effort and costs associated with utilizing a single parent captive, Marsh Nexus is a cost-efficient vehicle that complements broader benefits strategies and supports multinational organizations in optimizing next-generation employee benefit programs.” – Paul Lewis, Mercer Marsh Benefits’ Multinational Advisory Growth Leader.

item.title: Mobilitas launches Mobilitas IQ
item.description: Commercial insurance provider Mobilitas Insurance announced the launch of Mobilitas IQ , a third-party administration service provider designed to meet the claims management needs of commercial fleet operations, transportation platforms and modern mobility ecosystems. The launch strengthens Mobilitas’ offerings, which brings together commercial insurance and claims handling solutions. Mobilitas serves a broad range of transportation and technology-driven businesses, including fleet operators, delivery networks, rideshare platforms, transportation companies and emerging mobility providers, including autonomous vehicle companies.

item.title: El coste emocional del liderazgo: mayor bienestar pero más estrés y soledad
item.description: Sin embargo, también son quienes experimentan con más frecuencia emociones negativas como el estrés, la tristeza, la ira o la soledad. Los líderes tienen una valoración más positiva de sus vidas y muestran mayores niveles de compromiso con su trabajo que el resto de los empleados.

item.title: BHSI ficha a César Morales Eguía para impulsar el negocio en el norte
item.description: Berkshire Hathaway Specialty Insurance (BHSI) ha confirmado lo adelantado hace unos días por el BDS: la apertura de una nueva oficina en Bilbao y la incorporación de César Morales Eguía como suscriptor de Responsabilidad Civil, con el objetivo de reforzar su presencia comercial en el norte de España.

item.title: «No hay nada más terapéutico que sentir que alguien entiende por lo que estás pasando»
item.description: Sara Sorribes se ha convertido en uno de los referentes más visibles de la importancia de cuidar la salud mental en el deporte de élite.

item.title: Scouring Strait of Hormuz for Mines Could Take Weeks, Security Firms Say
item.description: Ensuring the Strait of Hormuz is safe from mines could delay a return to normal shipping traffic by weeks following a deal to reopen the waterway, shipping and maritime security sources say. The operation by conventional minesweepers and state-of-the-art underwater drones could continue for 40 to 50 days before many insurance, shipping or oil companies are confident enough to sail through, according to assessments from five Western maritime security sources. “The threat of mines in the area remains a concern immediately as well as further down the line and mine-free routes need to be established.” It is unclear how many mines Iran may have laid in the strait, which handled 20% of the world’s daily supply of oil and liquefied natural gas before the war.

item.title: Nonprofit Hospital Entitled to Liability Cap But Not Immunity: NJ High Court
item.description: The CIA that provides nonprofit entities immunity from negligence claims if they are “organized exclusively for religious, charitable or educational purposes,” also sets forth an exception to immunity for nonprofit corporations organized “exclusively for hospital purposes.” In those cases, the law limits their liability “to an amount not exceeding $250,000, together with interest and costs of suit, as the result of any one accident.” The high court said there is no dispute that NCHC is not organized exclusively for religious purposes. (NCHC) is instead entitled only to a $250,000 cap on damages as provided by the charitable immunity law because it is “organized exclusively for hospital purposes,” the high court found in reversing lower courts on the immunity issue. A unanimous New Jersey Supreme Court ruled on June 10 that a federally qualified nonprofit health center that provides healthcare to underserved populations is not entitled to immunity from a patient’s negligence suit under the state’s Charitable Immunity Act (CIA) that shields entities “organized exclusively for religious, charitable or educational purposes.” Rather, Newark Community Health Centers, Inc.

item.title: The AI Medical Summary Explained: What They Are, How They Work, and Why They Matter in Claims
item.description: The difference isn’t luck or experience; it’s an AI medical summary that transforms how insurance carriers , legal teams , and TPAs cut through the noise to get to what actually matters. Medical report summary AI doesn’t just pull random sentences; it understands medical terminology, recognizes patterns across different document types, and presents information in formats that claims professionals can immediately use, such as AI medical chronologies , key facts in an AI medical summaries , or detailed medical record summaries per record. What sets modern medical record summary tools apart is the Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) model.

item.title: Cyber insurance limit adequacy under the microscope across Asia as average ransomware event cost tops US$5m
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: India sells up to 5% of GIC Re via share placing
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Descartes Underwriting gains Lloyd’s coverholder status via new Oak Global partnership
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Later life lending: building the fourth retirement pillar
item.description: Speaker: Emad Aladhal, director of retail banking Event: Later Life Lending Summit Delivered: 16 June 2026 Note: This is a drafted speech and may differ from the delivered version Reading time: 10 minutes In the years ahead, housing wealth will become an increasing part of how many people provide for their retirement. Speech by Emad Aladhal, director of retail banking at the Later Life Lending Summit. None brought up later life lending, or how they could use their home to help.

item.title: Descartes Underwriting teams with OAK Global to deliver parametric solutions via Lloyd’s
item.description: Descartes Underwriting, the parametric risk transfer specialist, and OAK Global, the specialist Lloyd’s underwriting company, has announced Descartes’ approval as a Coverholder at Lloyd’s, which will see the firm underwrite parametric policies through OAK Global’s Syndicate 2843, bringing new risks to the London market. As a Lloyd’s Coverholder, Descartes will now gain direct access to the Lloyd’s underwriting capacity, enabling its growth into new strategic markets, particularly the High-Net-Worth individual segment in the United States, which seeks innovative solutions much like parametric, for insuring against natural disaster events. “This designation represents a major achievement for Descartes, as it demonstrates the confidence of OAK Global and Lloyd’s as a whole in our underwriting expertise, governance framework, and operational capabilities,” commented Tanguy Touffut, Co-founder and CEO of Descartes Underwriting.

item.title: Guy Carpenter hires Tregidga from Compre, for legacy and sidecar structured solutions focus
item.description: Tregidga joins Guy Carpenter in London and the company said she will “lead on legacy deals and sidecar activity, strengthening our capabilities in two areas that continue to grow in strategic importance.” The reinsurance broker further stated, “With a background that combines legal training and extensive transaction experience, Connie brings additional depth to our structured solutions offering as we support clients through changing market dynamics.” Tregidga joins Guy Carpenter from legacy, run-off and specialty reinsurance group Compre where she held the role of Group M&A Director. Guy Carpenter, the global reinsurance broking firm, has announced the hire of Connie Tregidga as a Managing Director in its Global Structured Solutions team, with legacy deals and reinsurance sidecars a focus for her new role. Her expertise in retrospective and legacy solutions, while having worked through a major casualty reinsurance sidecar deal from the perspective of an investor participant and provider of exit solutions capabilities will position Tregidga well to assist Guy Carpenter clients in accessing new and alternative capital sources, in structured formats.

item.title: CRC appoints Goldstein to lead proprietary capacity, alternative capital partnerships division
item.description: CRC Group, a wholesale insurance services and distribution specialist, has launched a new Risk Solutions Division to advance its focus on developing proprietary capacity, capital partnerships and alternative risk solutions, including its reinsurance sidecars, appointing Bill Goldstein to lead the division. In his new position leading the capital partnerships focused division, Goldstein will work to develop strategic capacity, including proprietary structures such as sidecars. CRC Group has now launched its newly created Risk Solutions Division to bring initiatives like this under one roof, naming Bill Goldstein as Head of Strategic Partnerships, Capacity and Capital Solutions.

item.title: Westfield Specialty launches UK & Europe liability portfolio, led by Keith Knight
item.description: Westfield Specialty International, a specialty insurance provider, has expanded its casualty offering with the launch of a new UK and Europe liability portfolio. Ross MacDonald, Divisional Director, Casualty and Portfolio, Westfield Specialty International, commented, “At Westfield, we have the appetite and capacity to take on new risks to grow our book, and the launch of the UK and Europe liability portfolio is a key step in that strategy. The UK and Europe liability portfolio will underwrite a broad range of liability risks, supporting clients operating across multiple jurisdictions and legal environments.

item.title: Willis report finds cyber insurance covers majority of data breach and first-party losses
item.description: In its latest report, Cyber claims in focus – getting value from cyber insurance, Willis found that insurance covers more than 95% of average data breach losses and 90% of average first-party losses. The report also highlighted the growing role of third parties in cyber losses, with external organisations responsible for nearly 50% of data breach losses and 29% of first-party losses. According to Willis, data breaches remain the most frequently reported cyber insurance claims, with malicious breaches accounting for the majority of incidents.

item.title: McKinsey executive Pradip Patiath joins AXIS Board of Directors
item.description: AXIS Capital Holdings Limited has appointed Pradip Patiath, presently a Senior Partner at McKinsey & Company and a global leader in the firm’s Financial Services and Technology division, to its Board of Directors. Effective June 15, 2026, Patiath will join AXIS’s Board, serving on its Risk and Human Capital and Compensation Committees. Marston Becker, Chair of the Board at AXIS, commented, “Pradip is recognised as one of the foremost thinkers and practitioners in digital strategy in the financial services sector.

item.title: Ukraine Aims to Align Banks, Insurers With EU Rules by 2028, Central Banker Says
item.description: Ukraine is accelerating reforms to align its banking and insurance sectors with EU standards by 2028 despite wartime challenges, to buttress investor confidence and deepen economic integration with Europe, its central bank governor said. Banks are well capitalized, and the central bank is actively seeking to implement reforms required for EU integration, Pyshnyi said. Pyshnyi said the central bank is implementing an overhaul of Ukraine’s insurance sector to make it more transparent, stable, and attractive to investors.

item.title: US-Iran Deal Promises End to War but How it Will Work Remains Unclear
item.description: Doubts swirled around the U.S.-Iran interim deal to end the war in the Middle East as shippers said it could take weeks for confidence to return after any reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, and fundamental questions remained unanswered. To be sure, the deal is the most significant step yet to resolve the conflict, which has killed at least 7,000 people, mostly in Iran and Lebanon, and upended global energy markets. and Iran, although details have yet to be made public and both countries said a permanent truce was yet to be negotiated.

item.title: Lockton names Stéphane Lespérance Canada CEO
item.description: Lockton announced the appointment of Stéphane Lespérance as CEO of its Canadian business, marking a significant step in the broker’s efforts to expand its footprint in Canada. Lespérance joins Lockton after a 24-year career at Aon Canada, where he served as president since 2019 and was named president and CEO in 2025. He brings the experience, credibility, and strategic vision required to deliver a premier advisory experience for clients across Canada.” – Ron Lockton, Chairman and CEO, Lockton.

item.title: Pints AI raises $5.6 million for auditable AI
item.description: Pints AI , a Singapore-based provider of AI systems for regulated financial institutions, raised $5.6 million in a Pre-Series A funding round led by Tin Men Capital, with SBI Ven Capital serving as co-lead investor. Founded in 2021, its Autothought platform is used by 12 financial institutions across four countries, generating more than $10 million in collective savings. According to Pints AI, the platform connects to an institution’s core systems, routes tasks to different AI models—including proprietary small language models, frontier models, and open-source models—and creates an audit trail for every decision.
